review_text:
transition from a 1999 Ford F150 manual transmission which I have enjoyed since 2022 into something smaller and gets around 50 mpg.  My wife and I went to Carver Toyota in Columbus Indiana on 19DEC for a fact-finding mission just to see if I fit into a Prius. FYI a 6'7" 200 lb man just fits into a Prius.  We met Jalen who is an outstanding young man whom I would describe as extremely knowledgeable of all Toyota makes and models, friendly, sincere, efficient, effective and highly motivated during the rest of the month of December Toyotathon. He answered our questions in a patient, concise and very friendly manner and asked us pertinent, informative questions as well. 

Long story short, my wife and I ended up spending 4 .5 hours at the dealership. We enjoyed our interactions and are now proud owners of a 2024 Hybrid Cienna XLE and a 2026 Carola SE. Thanks to Jalen, Ashley and Richard for answering all our questions, asking us the right questions, finding the perfect vehicles within your inventory and making our relationship with Carver Toyota quite memorable. My wife and I have thoroughly enjoyed our time with you and we are elated with the absolutely beautiful vehicles sitting side by side in our garage as well as how personally and professionally you worked with us.

Merry Christmas!

review_text:
We are from Illinois. We purchased a 2015 Tacoma on 01/31/2023, 65 days later, we still have not received valid license plate. The temporary license expired March 17th, so the car is sitting in the driveway! I called and left messages but no call-backs yet. I even asked for the GM and Sales Manager, but was told they were both off today (04/06/2023). 
Today's message was to ask if the dealership is willing to reimburse us if we rent a car. 

The title department did not offer us any help. I spoke to a dealer here in Illinois last night and was told "if that was them, they would've offered a dealer's plate temporarily, mailed it in via FEDEX next-day delivery". 

Note that when we purchased this car, it was done over the phone. It was probably the fastest deal we've ever done. I did not score Sales, did not name Salesperson, but we've done some repairs already that we thought should've been fixed during their inspection. It was an easy commission but I do wish he made follow-ups after he made the sale to ensure everything is in place.

We were told originally that Illinois is backed-up 6-8 weeks in processing titles (we were told this for 2 weeks). Then, most recent, that Illinois rejected because of some odometer issue; and that they needed to get the previous owner to sign something; and that this person actually came in yesterday and signed it. 

In the meantime, I think the dealer should offer to send us temporary dealer's plate ASAP. We will wait and see.

review_text: my door problem assessed and fixed. Both times they told me they “couldn’t figure it out” and sent me home. The problem kept getting worse until this past week when the same back door of my van came open while I was driving down the road- with my 5 year old sitting in the seat next to it. I immediately called Evans in a panic  and they assured me they would figure out what was wrong. And guess what? They did. This is the third time I’d taken it in for the same problem. They then proceeded to tell me that my warranty that would’ve covered the repair was expired by 6 days and that “unfortunately” I’d owe $1,400 for the repair. Lenny, the Service Director, called me and stated they knew what was wrong this time because his mechanics ran the diagnostic test for the door, and he also ADMITTED that they had not run this test either of the two instances before when I’d taken it in for the problem. Instead of assuming the responsibility of the mechanics not running the proper tests as they should have, he blamed me as the customer. WHAT?! I have been a long-time customer of this place, and I’ve run into many great employees, but unfortunately this circumstance will never have me back in their door. I’m a lifetime Toyota owner and will always be, but I’ll drive as far as I need to find proper service and mechanics who actually know what they’re doing. Lenny went far above and beyond to fail his very justified upset customer.

review_text:
2015 Ford Fusion on their lot. I called and expressed interest in the car and after speaking with the salesman, I was told that the car had been serviced and was able to be delivered to my home. This was great news to me at the time as someone currently serving Active-Duty. However they told me I needed to make a "non-refundable" deposit of $500 dollars to secure the car. The car I was told I was securing was not the car I paid a deposit for however.

In the following days-- I reached out asking for more pictures of the interior-- Then I was ghosted, until I had to reach out to another sales rep in order to get a response from my original salesmen. I was then given three rushed pictures of the engine bay, the front drivers, rear drivers side, and rear of the car. This car was put on the market, I was told it was ready to go, and yet there was cups on the seat, and the floor and interior was filthy. I was given no more pictures of the car due to it being on the lift.

I found out that the car was now getting suspension work done, which is why there was such a long delay in getting those pictures-- Which was odd to me, since I was led by the salesman saying that the car was in good shape. I could understand a quick oil change, checking the fluids, and maybe some flushing not constituting a "Hey the car still needs to be serviced" and I would be understanding of that, however I was not told that.

Additionally, I followed up asking to get a video of the engine cold starting (and being able to see the color of the exhaust smoke) after the weekend had passed, I was then ghosted again for a few days, I was then told, that I couldn't be sent that because the thermostat also was being replaced.

I then called and spoke to the finance manager here about how I was mislead a vehicle, and why I felt that way, and that I could no longer trust purchasing the vehicle, he then said he would speak to my salesman in order to figure out what went wrong.

My salesman then an hour and some change after the phone call I had with finance then reached out to me saying that he wanted to figure out why I was no longer interested, and that he wanted to earn my business so he wanted to figure this out for me. I am not sure if he or the finance manager didn't understand what I had said, because I reiterated over text what I have wrote here, and then 2 more times he attempted to offer to "fix" the issue or transfer my deposit to another vehicle.

So if you read all this; or scrolled to the bottom for the key points
Don't buy a used car here
Don't as a member of the Armed Forces buy a used vehicle here
Don't waste your time at this dealership, I promise you there are better options than here, that have no issue being clear, providing pictures, videos, and have already serviced and deemed a vehicle ready for purchase before they post it for sale.

While on the phone, I asked how long it would take and he said 2 hours.  The service advisor that took my phone call was great!  He explained what the 30,000 service entailed in detail.  My wife ended up taking the Highlander to the 9:30am appointment and arrived on time.  She had planned to wait at the dealership for the two hours that we were told.  After three hours, nobody came out to give her any update.  The service staff appeared to be having a "carry-in" of BBQ, and the service advisor that checked her in walked by, so she asked if there was an update because she had an appointment she needed to get to and thought she'd have her mom come get her and run her to the appointment.  His response was "well you have to be back by 3pm because that's when we close."  He volunteered to then finally go back and check on her car's progress.  He came back and said another thirty minutes.  It took another hour and 10 minutes before it was ready, for a total of 4 hours and ten minutes!  Throughout the process the service advisor (DK) never proactively gave her an update nor showed any sympathy.  He offered to have her car run through the car wash, but she politely declined due to her time constraint.  We've taken our Toyotas there for quite a while ago and they people and service have typically been great.  It seems like there are a lot of new faces now and this past Saturday's experience was not close to what we would describe as "great service."

review_text:
with an apology and an updated price. We went in and were assisted by Nathan, the sales agent who apologized for what happened and also Austin, the GM. Austin was willing to listen to our side and apologized for the previous encounter the other day. He was also willing to work out a better deal with us due to some other concerns we had. I have update the rating from 1 star to a 4 stars from today encounter.

09/28/2025: This place has been nothing but a disappointment. We bought a new Tacoma through them a few weeks ago & have been searching for a new RAV4 Hybrid, so we returned a few times to negotiate the price. When we bought the Tacoma, we declined the Ziebart protection plan because we didn’t want to spend nearly $2,000 on something we didn’t feel we needed.
However, when we were looking at the RAV4 last week, our salesperson Nathan told us that Ziebart is not optional on the RAV4 although we didn’t buy it for the Tacoma, & that we would be required to purchase it this time. Since we bought the Tacoma through them, he offered to take $1,000 off, while still insisted that it was mandatory. We asked if this requirement came from Toyota, and he said no, it was a policy through their dealership. He told us directly that he could not let us leave with the RAV4 unless we agreed to buy the Ziebart, which we felt was absolutely ridiculous.
When we discussed the OTD price, it already included the cost of Ziebart, so we didn't proceed with the purchase that day due to a disagreement over the final price.
We went back again yesterday to look at a different vehicle after discussing a potential OTD price. While Nathan was helping another customer, the sales manager, Andrew, came out to talk to us as we were looking at the vehicle on our own. He explained the benefits of the Ziebart package and he was quite convincing. since we had already been told that the package was required, I assumed that the OTD price we discussed already included it.
I tried to explain this to Andrew, but that’s when his attitude shifted. He suddenly became very defensive and said he knew what our “tactic” was. He brought up the fact that we didn’t purchase the Ziebart package for the Tacoma, so why would it be included for the RAV4 at the current price? His tone made it sound like I was lying or trying to trick them into giving us the package for free. He even made a comment indirect but clear that if I wasn’t willing to pay the price, I shouldn’t be buying the car. First of all, I am not trying to use any tactic, your own staff clearly told me that I have to purchase the Ziebart in order to walk out with the car I want. Trying to make the customer sound like they are lying is just ridiculous and unacceptable.
I walked out because I felt disrespected by his remarks. Later, he texted me, asking if I would be ready to write a check if they included the Ziebart package at the OTD price we had previously discussed. When I expressed how disappointed I was with how everything was handled, there was no apology. Instead, they doubled down, saying that since I had told them multiple times I didn’t want the Ziebart, why would they include it in the OTD price?
Yes, I did complain about having to buy the Ziebart but it was also to clearly told before that it was not optional if I wanted the RAV4. So of course I assumed it was already included in the price. As a buyer, I have every right to negotiate the price of a vehicle. And as a seller, you have every right to decline. But there is no need to say things like, “You shouldn’t be buying it if you don’t want to pay the price.” That’s condescending and unprofessional. I had planned to buy two more vehicles through your dealership & was going to refer friends who are currently shopping for new cars, but after this experience, I will not be returning, & I definitely will not be recommending this place to anyone.

review_text:
Wayne Toyota was awful enough to warrant one...

I called regarding a new Toyota Sienna I found online; we agreed to a price and I placed a deposit to hold the vehicle until that evening when my wife and kids could go make the purchase. Despite them knowing we were coming, a grand opening party with 100+ corporate people filled the showroom. Despite them knowing we were coming, they did no prep work before hand and we sat there at a table for an hour before finance decided to make an appearance. By that time, my toddlers were disruptive enough where I left with the kids and let me wife sign for the vehicle as it was going under her name. She was explicit on telling finance she didnt want any extra crap added like warranties. Lone behold, he slipped in almost 7k worth of added stuff after my wife made a mistake by expecting honesty and evidently signed the document. It took me 48 hours of persistent calling to get somebody to remove those service agreements. Then, they STILL have the nerve to come back and claim that they shouldn't have kept us at that lower interest rate and needed to raise the rate or shorten our loan terms. This conversation occurred a week AFTER we signed a new contract. What?!? Even after appealing to the GM, I was stood up. It took a threat of returning the vehicle on the basis of failed financing that got them to finally honor the original loan terms. 

Furthermore, there was an entire feature advertised online that was not on the vehicle. In fact, its not on Siennas at all (but it is a Toyota feature). 

Before the loan issue was resolved I made a complaint with Rohrman Auto Group. They've never followed back up with me. I've never heard an apology from the GM (or any words for that matter).  No compensation or free service offerings. At a minimum, they could have apologized for not warning us about a grand opening party that night which caused a very distracting and sour car buying experience - but not even that. The sales and finance people we worked with are shady and dishonest, which is wild considering how easy we made it for them to earn commission on 60k. Clearly this dealership and automotive group are ok with that behavior. For that reason, they've earned their 1 star and won't receive our business again.

review_text: One to purchase a new vehicle.  We were told the vehicle we were interested in was being used as the shuttle and eventually took it for a test drive.  We decided we wanted to purchase the 2024 Honda Odyssey. Took another three hours to get down to how much the payment would be, explain MULTIPLE times that we did NOT want the extended warranty and that we were, at that point, short on time.  We ended up haggling over less than $1000 and asked for a rim to be repaired as well as a knick that was out of the dash. After three times going back and forth with our offer, the person that our sales guy kept talking to agree to our original terms after we threatened to walk. Another 20-minute wait for the finance manager to come out and again explain the options of the extended warranty, where we again said we were in a rush and that we just needed to get going with the purchase.  Salesman gave us two options, said he could deliver the car to us next day and we could come back in the future to repair rim and dash, or they could hold on to it and it would be done on Wednesday.  We agreed to let them hold on to it until Wednesday and expressed that we were pressed for time and Wednesday would fit our schedule.  Wednesday rolls around and we hear nothing.  So, at around 3 PM we call and are given the run around about being able to speak to someone who knows what’s going on.  Finally, speak to our sales guy, Travis, and are told they took the tire to repair and has not yet been returned.  We advised that we are on the way and will take the spare if need be since we have already canceled our insurance on this current vehicle and it is now owned by the dealership. We get there and Travis went to get Nick from financing who told us that they were not be able to deliver the vehicle to us today because they cannot deliver with a spare tire. I understood that and suggested that they contact the person who took the tire to see when he would be delivering the tire back so that we can take the vehicle.  Nick explained again that he would not be delivering the car today.  I explained that we would be out of town on Thursday and Friday and are unable to get the van, which is why we opted to get everything done and have it delivered today.  There never was a Thursday option mentioned, or we would have taken the van as is and figured out a time to bring in back.  Nick sat smugly and said basically he didn’t have any solution for us except he could deliver the van tomorrow, to which I explained that I would have the trade in WITH ME in another city, so not sure how that could happen. We asked his manager’s name to which he responded, “there’s a lot of managers here”. I asked for the highest manager’s name, to which he responded, “Greg” and asked me to sit down while he went to get him. I said that we were leaving, but could I have his last name, and Nick turned his back and walked away. I asked again and he didn’t respond and kept walking. This dealership is the most unprofessional dealership I have ever dealt with. I cannot imagine having any kind of service done by them and I will certainly NEVER purchase another vehicle from here.

review_text: sales manager - Andy Sparger - hastily botched a remote sale in progress with his arbitrary terms that were not clear to me or my salesman. The preowned salesman - a really nice guy - and the preowned sales manager had poor and inconsistent communication between themselves and with me for the sale of a car I was in the middle of purchasing, in which I already had money down. Ultimately the sales manager sold it to someone else while they still had my down payment because I could not respond fast enough, in less than 24 hours, to his immediate and unexpected demand for full immediate payment, shared at the literal end of the working day prior. This contradicted what the salesman had initially shared earlier and what he and I had agreed to. The miscommunication between the two employees - including about my times of availability to discuss issues related to my purchase - ultimately ended with them selling my reserved car immediately. A few red flags about the dealership led me to do my own research that night before speaking with the manager the next afternoon - when the car was sold to someone else -- namely: 1/ receiving texts from personal cell phones 2/ vague explanation about the proposed 'ghost sale' (and me questioning its legality) and then 3/ the unexpected demand for full payment immediately before picking it up. For me, the most biting aspect was that the sales manager insinuated as though I had caused great distress to the dealership by not answering his call while I was working (teaching!) in the morning and midday and assumed that I was trying to scam the dealership. (How would I be a scammer if I had already successfully deposited $2,000 and had provided my driver's license, in addition to securing a plane ticket to the region?!?!) This alone shows his lack of due diligence to me and his salesman. He even said that I had left the dealership without communication for days, which was completely false, as I spoke to him less than 24 hours after he had demanded new conditions for the sale at the very last minute the day prior, and I came with informed questions about the process. When we did speak the next afternoon, the sales manager said that he considered my transaction cancelled and had invited a new customer to look at the car in that moment, and ultimately sold it to them an hour later. While the dealership has agreed to refund my down payment the next business day, I cannot express how disappointed I am in the sales manager. He acted without tact to give me less than 24 hours to respond to his last-minute change to the transaction process. He judged me to be a scammer despite money I had put up alongside my identification documents. A good manager would take ownership for this mismanagement and miscommunication. He had the opportunity to take responsibility and tell the potential new customer that it was his mistake, the car is in fact under contract - because it was. He did not, for the sole purpose of making an immediate sale. I wonder what my salesman - who had a day off this day - thinks of this hostile takeover of his transaction and client? It was unfortunate to lose not only my time (spanning three days of contact) and my peace of mind in working with the sales manager, but also money spent on the plane ticket that is now non-refundable. If the dealership actually cared about customers, the sales manager could work to rectify the dilemma he engineered by offering to find a comparable car at a comparable price for me, to make good on our initial transaction. I sure hope the ownership and HR team of this dealership take the time to investigate the protocol and communication system of the preowned sales manager for remote sales, to better respect customers who have committed to a purchase with a down payment, and to respect their sales associates in the process. I expect to hear back from ownership on a way to rectify this situation.

review_text:
On August 3, 2022, I took my Honda Accord to Kokomo Auto World for service because the vehicle's check engine light was blinking, and the VSA and exclamation point warning lights were on. At stop signs and stop lights the engine hesitated and ran rough. The service representative that I initially dealt with flippantly said the blinking check engine light was really a "time to trade in indicator", prior to having any diagnostics completed.
After a technician ran tests another service representative came to me in the waiting room. She informed me that the tests showed codes PO304- cylinder 4 misfire and PO430- leaking injector- damaged converter, and presented a Repair Estimate of $8,783.67 and advised that the vehicle should not be driven at all, almost giving me another heart attack.
My wife and I were staying at a lake house in Rochester. I decided to drive the car back to the lake house, using the cruise control set at 62 mph. Once there I called the service manger at Valley Honda, my home Honda dealer in Aurora, Illinois. We discussed the codes, symptoms and the Repair Estimate issued by Kokomo Auto World. Valley Honda has the largest sales volume of all Honda dealerships in Illinois, and I have done business with them since 1999. The service manager from Valley Honda indicated that the service codes and Repair Estimate made no sense to him. We decided that I would drive the vehicle back to Aurora, Illinois in the same fashion as I drove from Kokomo to Rochester.
After we got back to Illinois, I took the vehicle to Valley Honda. Service code PO304 was obtained and a finding that #4 spark plug oil fouled, but the indicated repair was drastically different. Valley Honda replaced #4 plug. The codes were cleared and retested. The car has been running like new since Valley Honda completed the work.
The invoice from Valley Honda was $118.75 vs the Kokomo Auto Repair Estimate of $8,783.67. INCREDIBLE! Kokomo Auto World is the worst. Either incompetent or dishonest, or both. I WILL NEVER HAVE ANY FUTURE DEALINGS WITH KOKOMO AUTO WORLD!

Michael McGurn

review_text: maintenance. Beforehand I checked fluid levels and marked my tires to make sure they got rotated as I've been burned in the past at other auto shops. When they were finished, I was given a several page readout of everything that was performed. My wiper fluid was still almost empty and my tires had not been rotated as stated they were. I went back in and explained to a service dept man up front what I found and stated how can I trust that all the other items on the checklist were even done. I told him I want another tech to redo the entire checklist. Afterward, he came back and said that he had the service manager redo it. The tires were rotated this time. However, the lugnuts are on way too tight that I would be unable to change a tire if I get a flat, I smell a burning smell each day that I drive it and it has been over 3 weeks since that appointment, and some settings on my vehicle have been reset including a trip A mileage that I am unable to reset and get the total mileage back on the screen. Idk if the battery was unhooked at the appointment causing this to happen or if there had been some sort of software update. Regardless, Idk why still having a burning smell after over 3 weeks and I cannot trust to take it back there again if I was lied to by the original tech about simple checklist items being done. It's not a free new vehicle ToyotaCare maintenance 2 yrs or 25,000 mile. It's paid for in the price of buying the vehicle. I didn't pay to be lied to. And I'm sure Toyota wouldn't appreciate it as I'm sure they compensate dealerships participating in this program.

review_text: for $10,500.  On the way home from purchasing the car ran poorly and the check engine light came on.  We immediately contacted the sales representative we worked with, Ed Morgan, and he said we got a great deal and there was nothing Royal South could do.  We took it to a local shop and they diagnosed that the engine had blown a head gasket and needed a new engine and other related damaged parts.  We contacted Ed Morgan again with this shop's diagnosis and he didn't even respond, let alone offer to have the certified Toyota service shop to look at it and verify.  The car is currently in the shop and the bill will be $6000 to repair.  It is apparent that Royal South cleared the codes before we purchased to make it appear like the engine wasn't blown.  Also, we were manipulated into signing an "as is" agreement, which gets Royal South off the hook.  Since then, I have stopped servicing my Volkswagen with Royal East, since they are affiliated. review_text:
"reconditioning" fees to the advertised price of a used car.

Seemingly-underhanded methods like this are not the way to build a relationship "that will last generations."  Most dealers include the cost of prepping a car in the advertised price.  This seems sketchy, particularly for a Toyota dealership.

We thought we were ready to purchase a used vehicle after a test drive, even though the price was on the higher end of fair according to KBB and it wasn't exactly what we wanted, but close enough.  

They charge a "standard" additional $1,198 on top of the advertised price.  When I add another $1,200 to our price range, there are much better deals elsewhere.  

The $1,198, when added to the advertised cost of the car, pushes it out of the fair range and into the high-priced range according to KBB.  It's a great way to bait someone with one price only to switch it after a test drive.

I was hoping to purchase this car after seeing the records of exactly what they did to prep the car to warrant such ridiculous fees the next business day, but I don't appreciate the way they deceive customers with their pricing scheme.

review_text:
I tried to work something out with the dealer before posting these bad reviews, but they went dark on me.  I completed a survey that was emailed to me and Edward Akers (General Manager) reached out via email stating "I am sorry to hear your experience with us did not meet your expectations.  We value your business and would like the opportunity to resolve this issue."  I replied to his email on June 25 and followed up again on June 29, July 4, and lastly July 10.  I never received a response back.  Here were the issues we experienced:

1. I live 1.5 hours away from this dealer.  I knew the exact vehicle and model number I wanted, so when Team Toyota told me they had it, I left work to go get it that same day.  I asked for an out-the-door price before heading there, as I did not want to negotiate once I got there. The out-the-door price was emailed over to me.  After spending 6 hours at the dealer (see next complaint), they tried to get me to sign paperwork that was higher than the number provided earlier in the day.  

2. We arrived at the dealership around 4:30 pm.  We didn't leave until after 10:30 pm.  It should NOT take 6 hours to buy a car.  We didn't test drive the vehicle at all since we knew exactly what we wanted (and the vehicle wasn't ready for us to test drive anyway), and price was already negotiated and finalized.  They told us they were understaffed with the financing department. The staff knew that we were coming from far away, and nobody mentioned that waiting and coming the next morning would be a better option.   The employees kept telling us that we're next and that it should "only be 30 more minutes" and hours would go by. The lights in the dealership were turned off while we were still in the finance office.  All the managers left us there alone with the finance person, so when we discovered the pricing issue mentioned above, they made us feel like it was a big deal because the managers already left and there was nobody to talk to.  So they had to call and track down a manager for approval to correct the documents.  

3. The car wasn't ready until 10pm or later.  The car arrived at their lot that day.  Again, I was very clear on the exact car I wanted, and they knew we were coming from far away.  They also knew our ETA as I was in constant communication with the salesman. They let us see the car shortly after we arrived to make sure it was what we were expecting, but wheels weren't fully inspected / installed so we couldn't safety test drive or anything.  They took the car back to the shop to "finish getting it ready" and they didn't bring it back out to us until 10pm or later.  Again, if they had this much work to do on the car before we were allowed to take it, we would have come the next day.  Instead, the dealership poorly communicated with us every step of the way, and has done nothing to try and "resolve this issue" to use their words.

and is attempting to fix the situation. He is keeping me posted. The fact that he reached out, is working on making it better AND is keeping me posted is worth everything. I will update.

ETA (9/7/23): I called the dealership AGAIN and asked to speak to the GM Jason Akers. The operator asked why, I told her and she put me through, it rang and then hung up on me. I called back, and it rang and rang and then went to voicemail. I left ANOTHER voicemail.

ETA (9/6/23): I called the dealership to speak to the GM the day that they responded to my Google Post. The GM had left for the day. I left a message with my name AND phone number (said them both twice). NO RETURN PHONE CALL. As previously stated, they are only interested in their clients that buy new with warranty. DO NOT BUY FROM THEM!

This dealership is relying solely on their reputation because they sell LEMONS! I purchased a 2005 Honda Civic from them not even 3 weeks ago. Yes, it was sold as is - no warranty. However, I was told that this was common because of the age of the vehicle. I specifically asked if there was anything wrong with it. I was told no. I was told that it is in great condition. I am a single mom and helping another single mom find a reliable car. We wanted to go to a dealership in hopes that we weren't going in blind like we would if we bought from someone off Marketplace. Clearly this dealership sells LEMONS just to pad their sales and have no concern for their clients, their safety or just plain ethical behavior. In 3 weeks, I have had the following done to it: change temperature gauge, change radiator, change radiator cap, refill the coolant multiple times, filling the tires (they were at 16 PSI), and now find out the head gasket is bad. When my mechanic friend looked it over, he noticed that the underside of the car was really "rough" as if it is been left at a car yard for a while.

Besides this, when they try to sell their cars, they try to sell them on FB Marketplace for the LIST PRICE. But then when you show interest, then they try to tack on taxes, titles and fees. Talk about a bait and switch. Unless you are buying a brand new vehicle that comes with a full warranty, I do NOT recommend purchasing from this dealership. They are only looking to take your money. They truly fit the stereotype of a sleazy car salesman.

Nissan of Richmond on Thursday 12/14/2023. We had been in contact via texting/email with Drew (a salesman) on a new 2023 Rav4 Hybrid Limited since Tuesday 12/12/23. We called the dealership on 12/14/23 in the morning and spoke with salesman Josh to confirm availability of the vehicle before we made the 2 hour and 15 minute drive from Madison, Indiana. Drew had the day off. Josh assured us the vehicle was available and he would have it sitting out front for us to test drive along with a 2024 Highlander Hybrid Limited. We arrived at ~ 12:10pm as Josh was pulling the last of the 2 cars around to the front of the dealership. As we walked in the front doors, another employee asked if they could help us. We told him that we had just driven from Madison to look at the 2 cars. He confirmed that he was aware we had driven so far and was expecting us. My wife and I drove the Highlander first ~ 5 miles with Josh riding with us. We wanted to drive a Highlander to make sure the Rav4 was what we wanted - the salesman was aware the Rav4 was the main vehicle we had come to purchase. Upon our return from test driving the Highlander, we went over to look at the Rav4. There were 2 customers sitting in the vehicle! Needless to say, the dealership sold them the vehicle while we waited at Josh's desk. The dealership should have told the other interested party that another couple had driven a long distance to decide on the Rav4 and they needed to give us the first opportunity to begin negotiating on the Rav4. Instead, we set at Josh's desk and got madder and madder as we watched the other people negotiating. We explained to Josh that this wasn't fair. He went to the managers desk to inform him of our thoughts and then just hung out at the desk while we just sit there and got angrier.
This wasn't fair to us whether we drove 200 miles round trip or not. That just added to the frustration we felt. We do not recommend this dealership. We were treated unfairly and they did not care. Again, we do not recommend this dealership.

review_text: Went for a service job scheduled my appointment a week in advance and called the day before to make sure everything was OK before going in. I dropped my car off speaking with service helper named Nerri.  Explained to me it was no problem and would take only 2 hours. He mentioned to me that they were apparently 6 cars behind.  I said no worries just call me when its ready. I waited 2 hours and got the call to be told they made a mistake. The part ordered for service was never ordered somehow even tho before the appointment it was checked in inventory and green lighted.  I demanded to speak to a manger, and was put on hold to be hung up on and then texted that they'll call me shortly.  As im on my way to retrieve my vehicle the manger tells me " so we have this story " , but this is no story and this is not a manger who is doing thier job.  As they crash out on me on the phone and ask me " what do you want me to do here." This shop is a joke , this was my second experience of using them in 6 months time. The first time was an oil change on my 2004 highlander. There was a 2025 grand highlander had the same thing done parked next to mine. As the owner drove out I watched 6 or more quarts of oil spill out the forgotten drain plug and watched them drive out and down the street.  Should have never came back but sadly I did. Stay away from this stealership and thier Unprofessional service station unless you want to pay thousands in repairs or have your vehicle held against you.

review_text: I liked a car, and a sales representative named Jeff Velasquez offered a 2025 RAV 4 Limited for $38,000. I was still undecided about the deal. I finally had a day off yesterday, Veterans Day. I called and got confirmation that the price was still the same. I went in to test the car and wanted to trade in my car as part of the purchase. They offered a very low price for my trade-in until I showed them the report from CarMax. They raised the offer a bit and explained the tax implications before and after the trade. I was aware of how it worked. Then they raised the price further. What a rip-off. You don't want to waste your time with this sales representative or his manager, who tried to convince me to come in for a test drive and made me wait. It was a complete waste of time. Their customer service is very poor, and they blatantly BIG LIED to customers like me.

review_text:
nothing but issues. 

I thought a used KIA from the Andy Mohr Toyota location - it had outstanding service recalls on it. The AC was not functioning properly - They refused to fix it and honor the listed price on the vehicle. They “met me in the middle” and took about $700 off the price but would not inspect or diagnose the issue without me paying THOUSANDS more than the listed price. 

In desperate need of a vehicle, I agreed to that. I took my vehicle to a local KIA dealership where they fixed both recalls and gave me a full inspection of the car - Not only did the AC need over $1000 worth of work, the cabin air filter was filthy, the oil filter was barely attached and leaking, the drive belt needs replaced… I am just absolutely disgusted with the outcome of this vehicle. 

The salesman acted bored the entire time bc I wasn’t buying a brand new Toyota - He didn’t know anything about the vehicle. And the service department clearly isn’t willing to help. 

I love my KIA and bc of this will NEVER shop at an Andy Mohr location, nor will I ever buy a Toyota.

review_text: I purchased a 2020 Ford Explorer ST from them. Everything seemed good other than a check engine light which they agreed to fix. Doesn’t seem bad right? After joined forms and Facebook pages about the Explorer I found out that it’s been tuned. The “fixed” the check engine which was a purge valve. Well it’s back, I inform them of it and that I want the PCM(computer) returned back to stock. They said the intake and the tune are messing it up and they will not fix it. Nor will they return it back to stock. Because it could “fry” the PCM. Which is false. They just don’t know how to do it. After talking with Ryan on the phone he told me it’s my fault because I knew the car was heavily modified, which was never once mentioned to me, it sounded like it had an exhaust but I have never heard one running before so I couldn’t tell the difference between aftermarket or stock. Then said I refused a warranty, a warranty was never offered because a warranty company wouldn’t cover a modified vehicle. Straight scummy business and the lack of ownership of the situation is crazy. Instead of just fixing their own screw up, they want to play games and point the finger. You sold the car that shouldn’t have been sold the way it was. I will be contacting the BBB and the Toyota regional manager to help with this issue. Not only does it tarnish the dealership but Toyota itself for the disregard in pride for their used auto satisfaction. Now I’m sitting here with a car that more issues will arise from the failing EVAP system which will cause even more issues down the road. Andy Mohr how can you have pride when all you sell is disrespect.

review_text:
I asked what this was for but the salesperson and I were interrupted by someone and the question remained unanswered.  I'm now financing that $6k but I've not received anything for that $6k.  The way I see it, Andy Mohr simply stole $6k from me.  I spoke to Cari Price later that week and was told that ALL hybrids have an upfit charge but this is not true. Just this week I've found two Rav4 Hybrids on Andy Mohr's Website that do not have this chg.  After the fact, I was told two different answers as to why that chg is added - the gal who answered my call said it was because of low inventory.  Cari Price told me it is b/c it's a hybrid - all hybrids are charged with it, which is not true as noted above. I was also told by Cari Price that the Buyers Order that I signed shows the breakdown of the total price.  It does not.  It shows only the higher price as the 'selling price' with the $6k already added in - it is not broken down anywhere on that form.  My call w/Cari ended with her hanging up on me - nice! 

One more thing I'd like to mention, after realizing I'd simply 'donated' $6k to Andy Mohr, I checked several of the other Toyota dealerships in the area.  Only one was charging a dealer upfit - but it was $2,000, not $6,000.  Not one of the other Toyota dealerships I checked were charging this upfit chg at all.  And yes, I realize I went ahead and purchased the vehicle without getting an answer to my question re: the $6k - I get it, that's on me.  But sales people have a way of distracting a customer and engaging them in other topics about said vehicle and before you know it you're in the finance office, unknowingly financing an add'l $6k for which I had nothing to show.  I woke up the following morning realizing the numbers didn't make sense and immediately called Andy Mohr wanting to return the car.  I was told by the finance guy (Braden?) that they would not take the car back - he had already filed the pprk w/Toyota Finance which I question that he'd really done.  It was close to 7:00 pm by the time I'd left the dealership so I question that he'd filed the pprk at that late hour.  Perhaps he did, but??

Having said all that, I will never again do business with Andy Mohr Toyota.  Nor will my kids - we have all driven Toyotas since the mid-nineties and we will likely continue driving Toyotas.  But we won't be purchasing them from Andy Mohr.  I have purchased so many cars along the way but never have I had the ugly experience that I had with Andy Mohr.

review_text: ! !  Drove 2.5 hours to look at a Tacoma and was planning on trading in a 4Runner. Told salesman I did not want any of it and wouldn’t accept offer on my trade.  Salesman said I had to take it and I told him that was illegal. I said I did t want any of it.  Sales manager (TJ I believe) said they would take $500 off and I said no and he went to his manager to see what he says and said they couldn’t do the deal without me accepting the optional add one so I said ok and left.  What kind of business practice is this and how many people don’t realize these numbers on the worksheet.  One thing TJ said when he first sat down was “Let’s not focus on the numbers, let’s focus on getting the deal done”.  Seriously?  The numbers are the deal.  You do not have to accept a optional add ons if you don’t want them.  Considering filing a report with the BBB.  This is a prime example why people hate going to car dealerships.

review_text: these guys deserve it. I put in multiple inquiries to dealers in my region. All of them played the usual games ("Do you have a trade? What do you want your payment to be?"). When I contacted Jeff Wyler Honda, there was none of that. I told them the vehicle I wanted, trim, options, etc. They were able to locate that exact vehicle. They quoted me a fair price right off the bat. Let’s face it - nobody is thrilled about spending thousands of dollars on a new vehicle. Thing is, the car buying process has changed drastically over the past 20 years, given the amount of information available to consumers online and being able to know the general range of price one should pay. That said, these guys have to make some money. I was not about to haggle over a few hundred dollars when given a fair price up front and treated well. The whole process was first class - I made a deposit to hold the car over phone/text, and came by for a test drive a week later (used version of what I ordered). They kept me updated on the car's status (coming over on the boat from Japan). When it arrived, I was notified that same day, and was able to come over that afternoon and take care of paperwork in just over an hour. Everything was exactly as they said it would be. Buying a vehicle doesn’t have to be an unpleasant experience. I was served by both Ben Jackson & Eric Meredith. I should also note: This dealership is about 1.5 hours from where I live, and there are several who are much closer. It was worth it. When we’re in the market for another vehicle, we’ll be calling Jeff Wyler Toyota. Give Ben or Eric a call - they will take good care of you.

review_text: Two hr. drive it was the only one available within 500 miles with what I wanted. When it came time to sign the purchase agreement there were added charges. 1. Appearance Pro Pkg. $400 told it was paint protection, no other info. could have been trip to car wash. 2. Wyler Certified Pulse $499 told it was a safety item that makes your third brake light pulse. Makes your light flash 3 times. Internet search shows that this brand is only sold to dealers who charge what they want. You can buy the same module that plugs into the brake light wiring for $25. 3. Wyler protection (policy cost only) $299 Told  it was insurance for the interior stains, tears. Received a booklet a month after car purchased it is a third party company. They list insurance deductible, ambulance service, tire hazard, lockout, and other useless services. They pay small amounts  per occurrence with a max amount per policy term,36 months. The kicker is  you pay for service and send in paper work/receipts with a reimbursement request and hope it is ok'd. Total for items that I would never ok'd if I had known about ahead of time $1198 Very deceptive business  practice. I have noticed different makes of cars with Wyler emblems with the pulse brake light so all Wyler locations must do this and probably other dealers. I have called Wyler corporate office 7 times and toyota of clarksville 3 times leaving contact info. and reason for call, today Dec. 30 still no return call. Would give negative stars if possible.

review_text:
Sienna Elsewhere

I really wanted to like this dealership, but my experience has been frustrating. They promised a “free tank of gas” in exchange for a positive review — something I never received — so keep that in mind when you see their 5-star ratings.

It’s been about ten months since I purchased my 2025 Toyota Sienna, and the vehicle has spent multiple months in their service department. Almost every repair has required return visits because issues weren’t fixed correctly the first time.

I’ve owned many vehicles, and this has been the least reliable service experience I’ve had. I expected far better from a Toyota dealership.

I hope management and Toyota corporate take this feedback seriously and make improvements. Until then, I’d strongly recommend buying your Toyota somewhere else.

review_text: I took my Prius in for a service/ multiple point inspection . Per the inspection when I left the dealership everything was Ok. He said the only thing that it required was a cabin air filter. I told him that was odd because I had just changed the cabin air filter myself a couple days prior. When I got home I went to change a headlight bulb and noticed the engine coolant reservoir was bone dry. I rechecked the paperwork they had given me and right enough it said they checked it and it was ok. When I pointed this out to Service advisor Hoagland he wanted to charge me another 175 to do a diagnostics completely ignoring the fact that their technicians were not really doing an inspection and just saying everything is ok. That was strike one for me. My wife has the Wyler package for oil changes and called to schedule one. They told her that she also had a recall on the car and to plan to drop the car off for 4 hours to get the service and the recall done. They even told her they hadn't had the parts for some months but now everything was in.I had to take a day off from work to drop her off and then to pick her up 4 hours later. When we went to pick up the car we were told that the car qualifies for the recall and that we have to bring the car back and now the recall would only take 1.5 hrs. When asked to speak to a Manager we were approached by Jason Purcell who non chalantly stated that he would notify his staff not to tell customers that. He was very dismissive to the fact that I had a wasted day at work for their error. When I tried to inform him about the issue I had with my car he turned around and told me " if youre so unhappy with our service why did you bring your car here" . As I was leaving I noticed that the maintenance required message was not cleared from the dash board.When I went back and asked him about this he rudely told me to learn how to read the manual and clear it myself. I told him I didn't pay the dealership for me to be doing this. Jason Purcell is extremely rude and condescending. When asked if there was someone higher than him he said he was the service manager!! However when I got home his card reads Assistant Service manager. So not are they condescending to paying customers but also liars!! Beware they dont really do what you pay them too.

review_text: share my experience from today in hopes it will help someone else make their decisions differently. I was looking at vehicles online and came across a very nice Town and Country, the van was modern, updated and had screens that the kids would love! I am 9 months pregnant and needed a bigger vehicle for the expanding family. I was sold for the price and immediately reached out and worked with David on it. I live almost two hours away from the dealership but due to the condition of the vehicle and price I could not pass up on the deal so I made the trip as soon as I could to get the vehicle. I scheduled an appointment with David, which was suppose to be for 11am (all texts I received showed my appointment was for 11pm which I thought was strange). I show up at 10:45am to be told that David was not in until 12pm. I was a little confused but they had me work with another person named Brian. At this point David from the previous day already had my info, ran credit, and gave me a pricing sheet with information on the vehicle, including a picture of the vehicle. When Brian pulled up the vehicle for a test drive I was surprised, the vehicle was not the same as in the pictures that they advertised or even on the pricing sheet sent to me… they advertised a Town and Country using newer and more updated Pacifica photos, a completely different model vehicle so it wasn’t a case of them just being good at taking photos to make it look nice, this was a completely different vehicle. I immediately asked Brian why the vehicle was advertised as a completely different vehicle by the photos and he stated he was unsure. He checked on it and found it was a mistake and that the Town and Country was posted with the wrong photos and pricing but management would make it “right” for me. I was devastated due to driving almost two hours to get that experience and sadly it didn’t end there. I then was told the vehicle of the pictures I saw was there and I even was given a test drive. I was excited and I was hopeful things were going to work out, but then when we come back to the dealership to start paperwork, I was told the managers didn’t communicate with each other and that the vehicle was actually already sold. To say I was frustrated, upset, and really disappointed was an understatement. I had drove two hours for false advertisement and pricing and then given a test drive for the intended vehicle to then be told it actually wasn’t available. I again was told, however, they would make it “right” for me. They were able to find a comparable Pacifica at a different location (many key features I was sold on though this Pacifica did not have such as screens for the kiddos). At this point, because of the distance I had traveled and hours already spent there, I felt like I really couldn’t leave empty handed. I know I could have left at any time but I also was told on numerous occasions that they would make it “right” for me so I was hopeful the experience would turn around. When it came to financials, there was no “right” done. The Pacifica they had found for me was 1/3 more of the original cost I was intending to spend on this vehicle based off their advertising and this one even had less features…So when it came down to paperwork I was caught off guard in seeing pricing was not considered to be made “right”. At this point I had been there from 10:45am - 3:30pm, not including my drive time, exhausted and ready to be done so I signed the paperwork for the vehicle and left feeling defeated. It got worse when I left the lot to get food and immediately noticed there was a chip in the windshield and the back right tail light was busted. Due to the long day and feeling flustered with the process I missed this when test driving (there were stickers on the windshield which could of contributed to hiding the chip) which is my fault for not noticing but this shows the quality of the vehicles they sell…

review_text: state. I told the salesman that I would finance through them, only if they were fair to me because we have been screwed in the past. We were trying to buy a brand new minivan. I sent him my trade in info and he was very pleasant to work with. I was not happy with the trade in value offered to me, but was willing to accept it because we really wanted this van. He then went through my payment options which seemed way too high. I asked what my interest rate was and he said he couldn’t tell me that. So I told him I was going to check with my local bank. Local bank gives me 2.49% interest and a payment over $100 lower a month. So I told him I was still interested in the van but we would be doing our own financing and just needed a purchase agreement. He then comes back and says his finance manager will not accept outside financing due to covid because “it takes too long.” I assured him that I will have a check by Saturday for the van, when we had discussed picking up and offered to put a deposit down today.  He says he’ll talk to the finance manager and get back to me. In the meantime, I call my bank and she tells me what they are trying to do is illegal, refusing to accept financing from an outside lender. Imagine not accepting a cashier’s check for an agreed upon price and blaming it on covid.... At this point we started researching this dealership and it turns out this is a common practice. We backed out. I was willing to travel 16 hours and let them make money off of me on my trade in AND their van, and that wasn’t good enough. I’m only sorry I wasted my entire day on this. Do your research, if you finance with them, you’re spending TOO MUCH MONEY. This is a shady practice. I never dreamt we could be screwed over by an official Toyota dealership while buying a brand new car. I guess I was wrong.

review_text:
Carvana and Vroom are becoming so popular. I spent more than 2 hours there with them refusing to return the keys to my car. 
I was looking at, and wanting to purchase a 2021 Rav4 Limited for my wife. We decided on the vehicle and proceeded to discuss numbers. I advised what I was willing to pay and the value expected for my trade. I advised my salesperson that these were what was required to earn my business. After 2 trips between the office and me, I ask for my keys, as it was clear they were not willing or able to meet the numbers I requested. 
The Sales Manager came out to discuss and I again ask for my keys. He advised he was unaware of what numbers I was looking for (I had clearly written them on the first offer). I gave them to him again and he agreed to meet those terms and ask that we start the credit application. 
We completed the credit application and they brought back the sell sheet with completely different numbers, while trying to add in several packages I had already said I did not want. Now they had hit my credit (five inquiries), and had lied to me about agreement on the offer to purchase. They still refused to give me my keys. The visit ended with me loudly advising other potential customer that they should leave before getting screwed with like I did. 
I would not recommend this place to someone I didn't like. Toyota should look at more than just the sales numbers and review dealerships tactics. This experience has ensured that I am not buying a Toyota and will never buy from a Jeff Wyler dealership.

review_text:
Low internet pricing: their advertised low price on the internet attracted me to this dealership. During email negotiations, the dealership lowered the price further. The multiple email communications prior to my onsite visit seemed disorganized, but adequate, or so I thought. In contrast to the apparently acceptable experience prior to my visit, my onsite experience to actually purchase the vehicle revealed misdirection, deception, lies, and unpleasantness. 
Misdirection:
Shopping from out of state, I tried to get as much information as possible about the deal before arrival. Five days before arrival, I asked about financing options, but I received delays and vague excuses, and I arrived on site without specific info on financing. I had stated that I would take the rebate instead of Toyota’s special financing offers (only one can be received), but Sales rep Kevin Lutz had mentioned in his emails Toyotas “0% financing” as though to imply that I could possibly receive both the manufacturer's rebate AND the 0%. I wasn’t going to count on both, so I had arranged my own financing. On site, I was ready to pay for and accept the vehicle, but I was told that if I accept the manufacturer rebate, then not only was I not eligible for Toyotas special financing offers, but also that I COULD NOT EVEN PAY WITH MY OWN FINANCING. In addition, I MUST accept the "CAPTIVE FINANCING" BY THE DEALERSHIP – which was higher than the 3% financing I had already arranged.  Then they printed and highlighted one statement buried among the very first emails they sent me, in which this restriction was found at the bottom of the message in the fine print. This email message was separate from the 7 other email conversation streams that the dealership had created in their communications with me, and the statement they referred to was NOT contained in any of the other email conversation streams in which they sent me successive offers. Jeff Wyler Toyota had misdirected me in several ways:  by creating multiple email conversation streams to obscure an important single statement that limited my financing options and cost me more; by delaying for 5 days giving me truthful info on financing; by mentioning the 0% financing despite my clear statement that I would be taking the rebate; then by showing me only after I had arrived from out of state that if I accepted the manufacturer’s rebate, I was obligated to their more expensive Captive Financing.
Deception:
During the discussion about the “captive financing,” I showed the Sales Manager an email of the offer made to me for the vehicle that contained no mention of the captive financing. The Sales Manager appeared surprised, then said, "I wasn’t aware that you had received this. I'll take care of it." He went to his office; 10 minutes later the Kevin returned with a smile and an apparent resolution to our dilemma: "He said to give you the 0% financing." Kevin did not mention any other changes to the deal. So this implied that as a result of the email I showed him, I would get the agreed price AND the zero percent financing – I was ready to sign up! Only upon my careful review, the numbers showed that the Sales Manager had REMOVED THE REBATE from the paperwork without mentioning it (!). Deceptive.
Lying:
Finance Officer C. Coomer (who seemed unhappy with her work) told me, “I am legally required to give you the following information.” I asked that since I had a long drive ahead of me, and I had already been there 2 hours, can she stick to the bare minimum? She looked at me with a serious expression, and repeated, “I am required by law to go over this.” I said OK and she proceeded. The “legally required” information was to offer upgrades from the $299 “Wyler Protection Plan” (which I didn’t want but couldn’t refuse), to higher-priced plans. In other words, Chelsea claimed that there was a LAW that required her to try to upsell me, from one overpriced “protection plan” to those even more overpriced. She should just make the pitch without lying.
Unpleasantness:
Ms Coomer had told me I would get “hard copies” of my loan agreements. After completing the paperwork, she gave me a thumb drive which apparently had all the documents for the sale. Thumb drives can fail or be corrupted, so I asked for hard copies of the loan agreement. She replied, “I gave them to you.” I said I had the thumb drive but not hard copies. She said, “The thumb drive IS the hard copy.” (?) I said by hard copy I meant a paper copy, and could I have a paper copy of the loan agreement? She glared, then bruskly told the Kevin, “Go make some copies for him.” As he left, Ms Coomer worked on her computer as I stood in her office waiting for Kevin’s return. After about 10 seconds, Chelsea turned to me, “You need to leave my office. I am working on other customer paperwork.” I was happy to exit her office to wait in the garage, a relief compared to the unpleasantness of being in her office.

showed that the sales manager had removed the rebate from the paperwork without mentioning it (!). deceptive. lying: finance officer c. coomer (who seemed unhappy with her work) told me, “i am legally required to give you the following information.” i asked that since i had a long drive ahead of me, and i had already been there 2 hours, can she stick to the bare minimum? she looked at me with a serious expression, and repeated, “i am required by law to go over this.” i said ok and she proceeded. the “legally required” information was to offer upgrades from the $299 “wyler protection plan” (which i didn’t want but couldn’t refuse), to higher-priced plans. in other words, chelsea claimed that there was a law that required her to try to upsell me, from one overpriced “protection plan” to those even more overpriced. she should just make the pitch without lying. unpleasantness: ms coomer had told me i would get “hard copies” of my loan agreements. after completing the paperwork, she gave me a thumb drive which apparently had all the documents for the sale. thumb drives can fail or be corrupted, so i asked for hard copies of the loan agreement. she replied, “i gave them to you.” i said i had the thumb drive but not hard copies. she said, “the thumb drive is the hard copy.” (?) i said by hard copy i meant a paper copy, and could i have a paper copy of the loan agreement? she glared, then bruskly told the kevin, “go make some copies for him.” as he left, ms coomer worked on her computer as i stood in her office waiting for kevin’s return. after about 10 seconds, chelsea turned to me, “you need to leave my office. i am working on other customer paperwork.” i was happy to exit her office to wait in the garage, a relief compared to the unpleasantness of being in her office.
